steps.title: Backsolving — testing choice B (116,300)
steps.content:
$$113{,}000 \times 1.29 = 145{,}770$$
This is far greater than 116,300, so choice B is too low.

steps.title: Fails. Testing choice C (142,000)
steps.content:
$$113{,}000 \times 1.29 = 145{,}770$$
This is still greater than 142,000, so choice C is also too low.

steps.title: Fails. Testing choice D (145,800)
steps.content:
$$113{,}000 \times 1.29 = 145{,}770$$
The projected number 145,770 is only 30 less than 145,800, making choice D the closest.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
